In addition, until you lose control of that objective marker, it has the Nurgle’s Gift ability as if it were a DEATH GUARD model from your army.

detachmentAbilities.name: Reverberant Rancidity
detachmentAbilities.legend: Reality rings like a struck bell as it rebels against the sheer wrongness of Nurgle’s daemons. Borne upon these jarring waves of metaphysical disease, magnified and redoubled with every dolorous peal, the Plague God’s myriad infections spread and multiply all the faster.
detachmentAbilities.description: While a Plague Legions unit from your army is within 7" of one or more DEATH GUARD units from your army, that PLAGUE LEGIONS unit has the Nurgle’s Gift ability. While a Death Guard unit from your army is within 7" of one or more PLAGUE LEGIONS units from your army, add 3" to that DEATH GUARD units Contagion Range. PLAGUE LEGIONS You can include Plague Legions units in your army, even though they do not have the DEATH GUARD Faction keyword. The combined points cost of such units you can include in your army is: Incursion: Up to 500 pts Strike Force: Up to 1000 pts Onslaught: Up to 1500 pts No PLAGUE LEGIONS models from your army can be your WARLORD.

detachmentAbilities.name: Numberless Horde
detachmentAbilities.legend: No matter how many Poxwalkers the enemy gun down or tear limb-from-limb at close quarters, more groaning mutants keep coming from every side.
detachmentAbilities.description: In your Command phase in each of the following battle rounds, depending on your chosen battle size, add a new Poxwalkers unit with a Starting Strength of 10 to your army, in Strategic Reserves. BATTLE SIZEBATTLE ROUNDSIncursion2,3Strike Force2, 3, 4Onslaught2, 3, 4, 5 KEYWORDS POXWALKERS units from your army gain the BATTLELINE keyword.

datasheets.name: Land Raider Proteus
datasheets.loadout: This model is equipped with: 2 twin lascannons; armoured tracks.
datasheets.transport: This model has a transport capacity of 12 Death Guard Infantry models. Each Jump Pack, Wulfen, Gravis, Possessed or Terminator model takes up the space of 2 models and each Centurion or Obliterator model takes up the space of 3 models.

options.description: This model can be equipped with one of the following:1 heavy bolter 1 multi-melta 1 twin heavy bolter 1 twin heavy flamer
This model can be equipped with 1 hunter-killer missile.
This model can be equipped with 1 storm bolter.
This model can be equipped with 1 explorator augury web.

abilities.name: Deadly Demise
abilities.description: DEADLY DEMISEFrom detonating ammo stores to corrosive innards or frenzied death throes, some targets are deadly even in defeat. Some models have ‘Deadly Demise x’ listed in their abilities. When such a model is destroyed, roll one D6 before removing it from play (if such a model is a TRANSPORT, roll before any embarked models disembark). On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by ‘x’ (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6"). Example: A TRANSPORT model with the Deadly Demise D3 ability is destroyed. Before any models disembark and before removing it from play, its controlling player rolls one D6, getting a 6. There are three units within 6" of that destroyed model, so its controlling player rolls one D3 for each of them, inflicting mortal wounds accordingly. Deadly Demise x: When this model is destroyed, roll one D6. On a 6, each unit within 6" suffers ‘x’ mortal wounds.
abilities.legend: From detonating ammo stores to corrosive innards or frenzied death throes, some targets are deadly even in defeat.

abilities.name: Nurgle’s Gift (Aura)
abilities.description: If your Army Faction is DEATH GUARD, while an enemy unit is within Contagion Range of one or more Death Guard models from your army, it is Afflicted (see below). CONTAGION RANGE Contagion Range changes over the course of the battle: AFFLICTED During the Declare Battle Formations step, select one of the Plagues below. Until the end of the battle, while an enemy unit is Afflicted, subtract 1 from the Toughness characteristic of models in that unit, and that unit has the effect of your chosen Plague.Skullsquirm Blight This horrifying affliction sees the minds and senses of the victim gnawed gradually away from within. Each time a model in this unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll. Rattlejoint Ague Limbs shuddering with fever palsy, bones turned brittle as glass, these warriors can barely act to defend themselves. Worsen the Save characteristic of models in this unit by 1. Scabrous Soulrot Victims of this insidious ailment become glassy eyed and morose as their very animus and essence decay. Worsen the Move, Leadership, and Objective Control characteristics of models in this unit by 1 (this rule can only worsen a model’s Objective Control characteristic to a minimum of 1).
abilities.legend: The Death Guard are warriors of the Plague God Nurgle. Their bloated bodies are riddled with corruption, and their mere presence causes the foe to sicken and wither in the grip of supernatural diseases while the battlefield falls to rot and ruin around them.

abilities.name: Explorator Augury Web
abilities.description: Enemy units that are set up on the battlefield as Reinforcements cannot be set up within 12" of this bearer, but the bearer must halve its Transport Capacity.

abilities.name: Assault Ramp
abilities.description: Each time a unit disembarks from this model after it has made a Normal move, that unit is still eligible to declare a charge this turn.

abilities.name: One Shot
abilities.description: The bearer can only shoot with this weapon once per battle.

stratagems.name: PUTRID DETONATION
stratagems.legend: Bloated with putrefaction and swilling with infectious poisons, the demise of this hulking entity is as revolting as it is explosively hazardous.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Any phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle or Death Guard Monster model from your army with the Deadly Demise ability that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that model even though it was just destroyed. EFFECT: Do not roll one D6 to determine whether mortal wounds are inflicted by your model’s Deadly Demise ability. Instead, mortal wounds are automatically inflicted. In addition, any enemy units that suffer mortal wounds as a result of this Stratagem are Afflicted until the start of your next turn.

stratagems.name: GROTESQUE FORTITUDE
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Battle Tactic Stratagem
stratagems.legend: After their champion calls down the blessings of Nurgle, these warriors can fight on no matter the catastrophic, often gruesome, damage done to them by the foe.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One Death Guard Attached unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, add 2 to the Toughness characteristic of models in your unit.

stratagems.name: COUNTER-OFFENSIVE
stratagems.legend: In close-quarters combat, the slightest hesitation can leave an opening for a swift foe to exploit.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has fought. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Your unit fights next.

stratagems.name: FONT OF FILTH
stratagems.legend: As though afflicted with some awful vomiting sickness, this vehicle's weapons retch an endless stream of fire at the enemy, heaving themselves empty even as it storms into the enemy lines.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [ASSAULT] ability.

stratagems.name: PLAGUESURGE
stratagems.legend: The foul miasma around the Death Guard swells and thickens, boiling outward like putrid smog.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Command phase. TARGET: Your Death Guard WARLORD that is on the battlefield. EFFECT: Until the start of your next Command phase, add 3" to the Contagion Range of models from your army.

stratagems.name: BLESSINGS OF FILTH
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Battle Tactic Stratagem
stratagems.legend: Employing their unique gifts, this champion enhances the weapons of their comrades with even greater levels of infectious malevolence.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Attached unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.

stratagems.name: STINKING MIRE
stratagems.legend: Attempting to counterattack Death Guard can leave foes floundering amidst bubbling filth.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Start of your opponent’s Charge phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time an enemy unit selects your unit as the target of a charge, subtract 2 from the Charge roll (this is not cumulative with any other negative modifiers to that Charge roll).

stratagems.name: SMOKESCREEN
stratagems.legend: Even the most skilled marksmen struggle to hit targets veiled by billowing screens of smoke.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One SMOKE unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, all models in your unit have the Benefit of Cover and the Stealth ability.

stratagems.name: RELENTLESS GRIND
stratagems.legend: When the armoured vehicles of the Death Guard advance, they plough through - or straight over- anything that stands in their way.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Movement phase or your Charge phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army that has not been selected to move or charge this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time your unit makes a Normal, Advance or Charge move, it can move horizontally through terrain features.

stratagems.name: TANK SHOCK
stratagems.legend: Ramming the foe with a speeding vehicle may be an unsubtle tactic, but it is a murderously effective one.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Charge phase, just after a VEHICLE unit from your army ends a Charge move. TARGET: That VEHICLE unit. EFFECT: Select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of your unit, and select one VEHICLE model in your unit that is within Engagement Range of that enemy unit. Roll a number of D6 equal to the Toughness characteristic of the selected VEHICLE model. For each 5+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound (to a maximum of 6 mortal wounds).

stratagems.name: EYESTINGER STORM
stratagems.legend: Swarms of these revolting insects burrow hives into the hulls of Death Guard vehicles. Drawn to sickened prey, they boil forth in buzzing masses and descend on the foe, spreading panic and horror.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Command phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army. EFFECT: Select one objective marker visible to one or more models in your unit. Each Afflicted enemy unit within range of that objective marker must take a Battle-shock test. Enemy units affected by this Stratagem do not need to take any other Battle-shock tests in the same phase.

stratagems.name: SHAMBLING WALL
stratagems.legend: Lacking even basic self-preservation instincts, Poxwalkers make excellent walking shields.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks, and one friendly Poxwalkers unit within 3" of your unit and visible to both your unit and the attacking unit. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time you would allocate an attack to a model in your DEATH GUARD unit, if your POXWALKERS unit is visible to the attacking model and is an eligible target for that attack, no saving throw is made for that attack; instead a number of POXWALKERS from your POXWALKERS unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that attack are destroyed.

stratagems.name: DRAWN TO DESPAIR
stratagems.legend: Those who languish in misery or cower in terror make themselves easy targets for the Death Guard.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets a visible enemy unit (excluding AIRCRAFT) within your opponent’s deployment zone, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

stratagems.name: SHOCK AND HORROR
stratagems.legend: Already dismayed by this living nightmare, the enemy falters before another sudden onslaught.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Charge phase, just after a Death Guard unit from your army ends a Charge move. TARGET: That DEATH GUARD unit. EFFECT: Each enemy unit within Engagement Range of your unit must take a Battle-shock test, subtracting 1 from that test.

stratagems.name: COUNTER-OFFENSIVE
stratagems.legend: In close-quarters combat, the slightest hesitation can leave an opening for a swift foe to exploit.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has fought. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Your unit fights next.

stratagems.name: EXPLOSIVE CLEARANCE
stratagems.legend: A well-placed explosive munition in confined shipboard spaces can cause bloody carnage.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase. TARGET: One unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. EFFECT: Select one model in your unit that is equipped with a weapon with the [BLAST] ability. Until the end of the phase, when determining how many models are in the target unit for the purpose of that ability, include models that are not visible to the attacking model. In addition, attacks made with that weapon can be allocated to models that are not visible to the attacking model.

stratagems.name: RABID INFUSION
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Strategic Ploy Stratagem
stratagems.legend: The combined talents of these Death Guard champions drive their followers into a foam-jawed fury, be it through injection with a curdled cocktail of stimms or immersion in a fug of infectious agents.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Start of the Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that includes two Character models. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, your unit has the Fights First ability.

stratagems.name: COMMAND RE-ROLL
stratagems.legend: A great commander can bend even the vagaries of fate and fortune to their will, the better to ensure victory.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Any phase, just after you have made a Hit roll, a Wound roll, a Damage roll, a saving throw, an Advance roll, a Charge roll, a Desperate Escape test, a Hazardous test, or just after you have rolled the dice to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon, for an attack, model or unit from your army. TARGET: That unit or model from your army. EFFECT: You re-roll that roll, test or saving throw.

stratagems.name: LEECHSPORE ERUPTION
stratagems.legend: Billowing from this warrior's wounds come clouds of parasitic spores that settle on the foe and drain their vitality, stealing it for the one who spawned them.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Command phase. TARGET: One Death Guard model your army that has lost one or more wounds. EFFECT: Select one enemy unit within 3" of your model. Roll a number of D6 equal to the number of wounds your model has lost: for each 5+, that enemy unit suffers one mortal wound (to a maximum of 6 mortal wounds) and your model regains 1 lost wound (to a maximum of 6 lost wounds).

stratagems.name: RAPID INGRESS
stratagems.legend: Be it cunning strategy, potent technology or supernatural ritual, there are many means by which a commander may hasten their warriors’ onset.
stratagems.description: WHEN: End of your opponent’s Movement phase. TARGET: One unit from your army that is in Reserves. EFFECT: Your unit can arrive on the battlefield as if it were the Reinforcements step of your Movement phase, and if every model in that unit has the Deep Strike ability, you can set that unit up as described in the Deep Strike ability (even though it is not your Movement phase). RESTRICTIONS: You cannot use this Stratagem to enable a unit to arrive on the battlefield during a battle round it would not normally be able to do so in.

stratagems.name: COMMAND RE-ROLL
stratagems.legend: A great commander can bend even the vagaries of fate and fortune to their will, the better to ensure victory.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Any phase, just after you make an Advance roll, a Charge roll, a Desperate Escape test or a Hazardous test for a unit from your army, or a Hit roll, a Wound roll, a Damage roll or a saving throw for a model in that unit, or a roll to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon equipped by a model in that unit. If you are using fast dice rolling, this Stratagem can still be used after rolling multiple rolls or saving throws at once. TARGET: That unit or model from your army. EFFECT: You re-roll that roll, test or saving throw. If you are using fast dice rolling, select one of those rolls or saving throws to re-roll.

stratagems.name: INSANE BRAVERY
stratagems.legend: Indifferent to their own survival, these warriors hold their ground against seemingly impossible odds.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Battle-shock step of your Command phase, just before you take a Battle-shock test for a unit from your army. TARGET: That unit from your army. EFFECT: Your unit automatically passes that Battle-shock test. RESTRICTIONS: You cannot use this Stratagem more than once per battle.

stratagems.name: CLUTCHING CORRUPTION
stratagems.legend: The daemons of Nurgle grasp their victims tightly with rotting talons and coiling tentacles, their clutch as inescapable as entropy itself.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit that is within Engagement Range of one or more Plague Legions units from your army, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

stratagems.name: FIRE OVERWATCH
stratagems.legend: A hail of wildfire can drive back advancing foes.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Movement or Charge phase, just after an enemy unit is set up or when an enemy unit starts or ends a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, or declares a charge. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within 24" of that enemy unit and that would be eligible to shoot if it were your Shooting phase. EFFECT: If that enemy unit is visible to your unit, your unit can shoot that enemy unit as if it were your Shooting phase. RESTRICTIONS: You cannot target a TITANIC unit with this Stratagem. Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a ranged attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 6 is required to score a hit, irrespective of the attacking weapon’s Ballistic Skill or any modifiers. You can only use this Stratagem once per turn.

stratagems.name: DISGUSTINGLY RESILIENT
stratagems.legend: Those favoured by Nurgle are inured to pain, their rotting bodies shrugging off all but the most traumatic damage with ease.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time an attack is allocated to a model in your unit, subtract 1 from the Damage characteristic of that attack.

stratagems.name: MALIGNANCE MAGNIFIED
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Battle Tactic Stratagem
stratagems.legend: The vile talents of such plague specialists act like a filth-smeared lens, focusing and intensifying the infectious gifts of Nurgle.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Attached unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets a unit that is below its Starting Strength, you can re-roll the Hit roll and you can re-roll the Wound roll.

stratagems.name: INSANE BRAVERY
stratagems.legend: Indifferent to their own survival, these warriors hold their ground against seemingly impossible odds.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Battle-shock step of your Command phase, just after you have failed a Battle-shock test taken for a unit from your army. TARGET: The unit from your army that Battle-shock test was just taken for (even though your Battle-shocked units cannot normally be affected by your Stratagems). EFFECT: Your unit is treated as having passed that test instead, and is not Battle-shocked as a result.

stratagems.name: BLIGHTED LAND
stratagems.legend: Firing diseased submunitions or belching swarms of daemonic parasites from its hull, this war engine befouls the battlefield as it advances.
stratagems.description: WHEN: End of your Movement phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army. EFFECT: Select one terrain feature within 24" of and visible to your unit. Until the start of your next turn, enemy units are Afflicted while they are within 3" of that terrain feature.

detachmentAbilities.name: Miasmic Bombardment
detachmentAbilities.legend: In the wake of preparatory bombardment by artillery weapons both forbidden and foul, the surviving foe are left reeling amidst clouds of plague smog. With their flesh running like tallow and their weapons rusting in their hands, they will be hard-pressed to fight back.
detachmentAbilities.description: At the start of the battle round, select a number of enemy units more than 12" away from every model from your army that is on the battlefield. Until the end of the battle round, those enemy units are Afflicted. The maximum number of units you can select in this way depends on the battle size, as shown below. BATTLE SIZEUNITSIncursion1Strike Force2Onslaught3

detachmentAbilities.name: Manifold Maladies
detachmentAbilities.legend: A spirit of competition infects Nurgle’s champions when they fight alongside one another. With each vying to outdo the others in virulence, the foes are subjected to a riotous string of nightmarish outbreaks amongst their ranks.
detachmentAbilities.description: At the start of the battle round, you can select one of the Plagues listed in Nurgle’s Gift. Until the end of the battle, that is your chosen Plague instead of any previously chosen Plague.

detachmentAbilities.name: Worldblight
detachmentAbilities.legend: As the foul tendrils of the Death Guard quest further across the battlefield, they infest even the most secure, holy and heavily warded sites with supernatural disease. To fight amidst such foulness is a virtual death sentence to the foe, win or lose.
detachmentAbilities.description: If you control an objective marker at the end of your Command phase and a Death Guard unit from your army (excluding Battle-shocked units) is within range of that objective marker, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase. In addition, until you lose control of that objective marker, it has the Nurgle’s Gift ability as if it were a DEATH GUARD model from your army.

detachmentAbilities.name: Numberless Horde
detachmentAbilities.legend: No matter how many Poxwalkers the enemy gun down or tear limb-from-limb at close quarters, more groaning mutants keep coming from every side.
detachmentAbilities.description: In your Command phase in each of the following battle rounds, depending on your chosen battle size, add a new Poxwalkers unit with a Starting Strength of 10 to your army, in Strategic Reserves. BATTLE SIZEBATTLE ROUNDSIncursion2,3Strike Force2, 3, 4Onslaught2, 3, 4, 5 KEYWORDS POXWALKERS units from your army gain the BATTLELINE keyword.

detachmentAbilities.name: Deadly Vectors
detachmentAbilities.legend: The plagues and parasites to which these elite warriors play host are amongst the most lethal in the galaxy. Moment by moment, even the most resilient foes sicken and wither in their presence until all that remains is rotted matter deliquescing sloppily from mouldering bones.
detachmentAbilities.description: In your opponent’s Command phase, roll 2D6 for each Afflicted enemy unit, subtracting 1 from the result if that unit is Below Half-strength. If the result is 6 or less, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds.

detachmentAbilities.name: Reverberant Rancidity
detachmentAbilities.legend: Reality rings like a struck bell as it rebels against the sheer wrongness of Nurgle’s daemons. Borne upon these jarring waves of metaphysical disease, magnified and redoubled with every dolorous peal, the Plague God’s myriad infections spread and multiply all the faster.
detachmentAbilities.description: While a Plague Legions unit from your army is within 7" of one or more DEATH GUARD units from your army, that PLAGUE LEGIONS unit has the Nurgle’s Gift ability. While a Death Guard unit from your army is within 7" of one or more PLAGUE LEGIONS units from your army, add 3" to that DEATH GUARD units Contagion Range. PLAGUE LEGIONS You can include Plague Legions units in your army, even though they do not have the DEATH GUARD Faction keyword. The combined points cost of such units you can include in your army is: Incursion: Up to 500 pts Strike Force: Up to 1000 pts Onslaught: Up to 1500 pts No PLAGUE LEGIONS models from your army can be your WARLORD.

datasheets.name: Fire Raptor Gunship
datasheets.loadout: This model is equipped with: twin avenger bolt cannon; 2 twin autocannons; 2 twin hellstrike launchers; armoured hull

options.description: This model’s 2 twin hellstrike missile launchers can be replaced with 2 twin lascannons
This model’s 2 twin autocannons can be replaced with 2 quad heavy bolters.

Some models have ‘Deadly Demise x’ listed in their abilities. On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by ‘x’ (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6"). Example: A TRANSPORT model with the Deadly Demise D3 ability is destroyed. Before any models disembark and before removing it from play, its controlling player rolls one D6, getting a 6. There are three units within 6" of that destroyed model, so its controlling player rolls one D3 for each of them, inflicting mortal wounds accordingly. Deadly Demise x: When this model is destroyed, roll one D6. On a 6, each unit within 6" suffers ‘x’ mortal wounds.
abilities.legend: From detonating ammo stores to corrosive innards or frenzied death throes, some targets are deadly even in defeat.

abilities.name: Hover
abilities.description: HOVERSome aircraft can use vectored thrusters or anti-grav technology to hover over the battlefield, the better to hunt their prey or deploy embarked troops. Some AIRCRAFT models have ‘Hover’ listed in their abilities. When you are instructed to Declare Battle Formations, before doing anything else, you must first declare which models from your army with this ability will be in Hover mode. If a model is in Hover mode, then until the end of the battle, its Move characteristic is changed to 20", it loses the AIRCRAFT keyword and it loses all associated rules for being an AIRCRAFT model. Models in Hover mode do not start the battle in Reserves, but you can choose to place them into Strategic Reserves following the normal rules if you wish.
abilities.legend: Some aircraft can use vectored thrusters or anti-grav technology to hover over the battlefield, the better to hunt their prey or deploy embarked troops.

abilities.name: Nurgle’s Gift (Aura)

CONTAGION RANGE Contagion Range changes over the course of the battle: AFFLICTED During the Declare Battle Formations step, select one of the Plagues below. Until the end of the battle, while an enemy unit is Afflicted, subtract 1 from the Toughness characteristic of models in that unit, and that unit has the effect of your chosen Plague.Skullsquirm Blight This horrifying affliction sees the minds and senses of the victim gnawed gradually away from within. Scabrous Soulrot Victims of this insidious ailment become glassy eyed and morose as their very animus and essence decay. Worsen the Move, Leadership, and Objective Control characteristics of models in this unit by 1 (this rule can only worsen a model’s Objective Control characteristic to a minimum of 1).
abilities.legend: The Death Guard are warriors of the Plague God Nurgle. Their bloated bodies are riddled with corruption, and their mere presence causes the foe to sicken and wither in the grip of supernatural diseases while the battlefield falls to rot and ruin around them.

abilities.name: Strafing Run
abilities.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack that targets a unit that cannot Fly, add 1 to the Hit roll.

stratagems.name: RAPID INGRESS
stratagems.legend: Be it cunning strategy, potent technology or supernatural ritual, there are many means by which a commander may hasten their warriors’ onset.
stratagems.description: WHEN: End of your opponent’s Movement phase. TARGET: One unit from your army that is in Reserves. EFFECT: Your unit can arrive on the battlefield as if it were the Reinforcements step of your Movement phase, and if every model in that unit has the Deep Strike ability, you can set that unit up as described in the Deep Strike ability (even though it is not your Movement phase). RESTRICTIONS: You cannot use this Stratagem to enable a unit to arrive on the battlefield during a battle round it would not normally be able to do so in.

stratagems.name: RABID INFUSION
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Strategic Ploy Stratagem
stratagems.legend: The combined talents of these Death Guard champions drive their followers into a foam-jawed fury, be it through injection with a curdled cocktail of stimms or immersion in a fug of infectious agents.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Start of the Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that includes two Character models. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, your unit has the Fights First ability.

stratagems.name: COUNTER-OFFENSIVE
stratagems.legend: In close-quarters combat, the slightest hesitation can leave an opening for a swift foe to exploit.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has fought. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Your unit fights next.

stratagems.name: FIRE OVERWATCH
stratagems.legend: A hail of wildfire can drive back advancing foes.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Movement or Charge phase, just after an enemy unit is set up or when an enemy unit starts or ends a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, or declares a charge. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within 24" of that enemy unit and that would be eligible to shoot if it were your Shooting phase. EFFECT: If that enemy unit is visible to your unit, your unit can shoot that enemy unit as if it were your Shooting phase. RESTRICTIONS: You cannot target a TITANIC unit with this Stratagem. Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes a ranged attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 6 is required to score a hit, irrespective of the attacking weapon’s Ballistic Skill or any modifiers. You can only use this Stratagem once per turn.

stratagems.name: DISGUSTINGLY RESILIENT
stratagems.legend: Those favoured by Nurgle are inured to pain, their rotting bodies shrugging off all but the most traumatic damage with ease.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time an attack is allocated to a model in your unit, subtract 1 from the Damage characteristic of that attack.

stratagems.name: SHAMBLING WALL
stratagems.legend: Lacking even basic self-preservation instincts, Poxwalkers make excellent walking shields.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks, and one friendly Poxwalkers unit within 3" of your unit and visible to both your unit and the attacking unit. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time you would allocate an attack to a model in your DEATH GUARD unit, if your POXWALKERS unit is visible to the attacking model and is an eligible target for that attack, no saving throw is made for that attack; instead a number of POXWALKERS from your POXWALKERS unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that attack are destroyed.

stratagems.name: EYESTINGER STORM
stratagems.legend: Swarms of these revolting insects burrow hives into the hulls of Death Guard vehicles. Drawn to sickened prey, they boil forth in buzzing masses and descend on the foe, spreading panic and horror.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Command phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army. EFFECT: Select one objective marker visible to one or more models in your unit. Each Afflicted enemy unit within range of that objective marker must take a Battle-shock test. Enemy units affected by this Stratagem do not need to take any other Battle-shock tests in the same phase.

stratagems.name: GROTESQUE FORTITUDE
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Battle Tactic Stratagem
stratagems.legend: After their champion calls down the blessings of Nurgle, these warriors can fight on no matter the catastrophic, often gruesome, damage done to them by the foe.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your opponent’s Shooting phase or the Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has selected its targets. TARGET: One Death Guard Attached unit from your army that was selected as the target of one or more of the attacking unit’s attacks. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, add 2 to the Toughness characteristic of models in your unit.

stratagems.name: MALIGNANCE MAGNIFIED
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Battle Tactic Stratagem
stratagems.legend: The vile talents of such plague specialists act like a filth-smeared lens, focusing and intensifying the infectious gifts of Nurgle.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Attached unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets a unit that is below its Starting Strength, you can re-roll the Hit roll and you can re-roll the Wound roll.

stratagems.name: STINKING MIRE
stratagems.legend: Attempting to counterattack Death Guard can leave foes floundering amidst bubbling filth.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Start of your opponent’s Charge phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time an enemy unit selects your unit as the target of a charge, subtract 2 from the Charge roll (this is not cumulative with any other negative modifiers to that Charge roll).

stratagems.name: DRAWN TO DESPAIR
stratagems.legend: Those who languish in misery or cower in terror make themselves easy targets for the Death Guard.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets a visible enemy unit (excluding AIRCRAFT) within your opponent’s deployment zone, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

stratagems.name: TANK SHOCK
stratagems.legend: Ramming the foe with a speeding vehicle may be an unsubtle tactic, but it is a murderously effective one.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Charge phase, just after a VEHICLE unit from your army ends a Charge move. TARGET: That VEHICLE unit. EFFECT: Select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of your unit, and select one VEHICLE model in your unit that is within Engagement Range of that enemy unit. Roll a number of D6 equal to the Toughness characteristic of the selected VEHICLE model. For each 5+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound (to a maximum of 6 mortal wounds).

stratagems.name: FONT OF FILTH
stratagems.legend: As though afflicted with some awful vomiting sickness, this vehicle's weapons retch an endless stream of fire at the enemy, heaving themselves empty even as it storms into the enemy lines.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in your unit have the [ASSAULT] ability.

stratagems.name: BLESSINGS OF FILTH
stratagems.type: Champions of Contagion – Battle Tactic Stratagem
stratagems.legend: Employing their unique gifts, this champion enhances the weapons of their comrades with even greater levels of infectious malevolence.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase or the Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Attached unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot or fight this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack, an unmodified Hit roll of 5+ scores a Critical Hit.

stratagems.name: INSANE BRAVERY
stratagems.legend: Indifferent to their own survival, these warriors hold their ground against seemingly impossible odds.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Battle-shock step of your Command phase, just before you take a Battle-shock test for a unit from your army. TARGET: That unit from your army. EFFECT: Your unit automatically passes that Battle-shock test. RESTRICTIONS: You cannot use this Stratagem more than once per battle.

stratagems.name: CLUTCHING CORRUPTION
stratagems.legend: The daemons of Nurgle grasp their victims tightly with rotting talons and coiling tentacles, their clutch as inescapable as entropy itself.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase. TARGET: One Death Guard unit from your army that has not been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time a model in your unit makes an attack that targets an enemy unit that is within Engagement Range of one or more Plague Legions units from your army, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

stratagems.name: EXPLOSIVE CLEARANCE
stratagems.legend: A well-placed explosive munition in confined shipboard spaces can cause bloody carnage.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Shooting phase. TARGET: One unit from your army that has not been selected to shoot this phase. EFFECT: Select one model in your unit that is equipped with a weapon with the [BLAST] ability. Until the end of the phase, when determining how many models are in the target unit for the purpose of that ability, include models that are not visible to the attacking model. In addition, attacks made with that weapon can be allocated to models that are not visible to the attacking model.

stratagems.name: COMMAND RE-ROLL
stratagems.legend: A great commander can bend even the vagaries of fate and fortune to their will, the better to ensure victory.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Any phase, just after you have made a Hit roll, a Wound roll, a Damage roll, a saving throw, an Advance roll, a Charge roll, a Desperate Escape test, a Hazardous test, or just after you have rolled the dice to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon, for an attack, model or unit from your army. TARGET: That unit or model from your army. EFFECT: You re-roll that roll, test or saving throw.

stratagems.name: RELENTLESS GRIND
stratagems.legend: When the armoured vehicles of the Death Guard advance, they plough through - or straight over- anything that stands in their way.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Movement phase or your Charge phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army that has not been selected to move or charge this phase. EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time your unit makes a Normal, Advance or Charge move, it can move horizontally through terrain features.

stratagems.name: INSANE BRAVERY
stratagems.legend: Indifferent to their own survival, these warriors hold their ground against seemingly impossible odds.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Battle-shock step of your Command phase, just after you have failed a Battle-shock test taken for a unit from your army. TARGET: The unit from your army that Battle-shock test was just taken for (even though your Battle-shocked units cannot normally be affected by your Stratagems). EFFECT: Your unit is treated as having passed that test instead, and is not Battle-shocked as a result.

stratagems.name: COUNTER-OFFENSIVE
stratagems.legend: In close-quarters combat, the slightest hesitation can leave an opening for a swift foe to exploit.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has fought. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Your unit fights next.

stratagems.name: BLIGHTED LAND
stratagems.legend: Firing diseased submunitions or belching swarms of daemonic parasites from its hull, this war engine befouls the battlefield as it advances.
stratagems.description: WHEN: End of your Movement phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle unit from your army. EFFECT: Select one terrain feature within 24" of and visible to your unit. Until the start of your next turn, enemy units are Afflicted while they are within 3" of that terrain feature.

stratagems.name: COMMAND RE-ROLL
stratagems.legend: A great commander can bend even the vagaries of fate and fortune to their will, the better to ensure victory.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Any phase, just after you make an Advance roll, a Charge roll, a Desperate Escape test or a Hazardous test for a unit from your army, or a Hit roll, a Wound roll, a Damage roll or a saving throw for a model in that unit, or a roll to determine the number of attacks made with a weapon equipped by a model in that unit. If you are using fast dice rolling, this Stratagem can still be used after rolling multiple rolls or saving throws at once. TARGET: That unit or model from your army. EFFECT: You re-roll that roll, test or saving throw. If you are using fast dice rolling, select one of those rolls or saving throws to re-roll.

stratagems.name: PLAGUESURGE
stratagems.legend: The foul miasma around the Death Guard swells and thickens, boiling outward like putrid smog.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Command phase. TARGET: Your Death Guard WARLORD that is on the battlefield. EFFECT: Until the start of your next Command phase, add 3" to the Contagion Range of models from your army.

stratagems.name: LEECHSPORE ERUPTION
stratagems.legend: Billowing from this warrior's wounds come clouds of parasitic spores that settle on the foe and drain their vitality, stealing it for the one who spawned them.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Command phase. TARGET: One Death Guard model your army that has lost one or more wounds. EFFECT: Select one enemy unit within 3" of your model. Roll a number of D6 equal to the number of wounds your model has lost: for each 5+, that enemy unit suffers one mortal wound (to a maximum of 6 mortal wounds) and your model regains 1 lost wound (to a maximum of 6 lost wounds).

stratagems.name: PUTRID DETONATION
stratagems.legend: Bloated with putrefaction and swilling with infectious poisons, the demise of this hulking entity is as revolting as it is explosively hazardous.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Any phase. TARGET: One Death Guard Vehicle or Death Guard Monster model from your army with the Deadly Demise ability that was just destroyed. You can use this Stratagem on that model even though it was just destroyed. EFFECT: Do not roll one D6 to determine whether mortal wounds are inflicted by your model’s Deadly Demise ability. Instead, mortal wounds are automatically inflicted. In addition, any enemy units that suffer mortal wounds as a result of this Stratagem are Afflicted until the start of your next turn.

stratagems.name: SHOCK AND HORROR
stratagems.legend: Already dismayed by this living nightmare, the enemy falters before another sudden onslaught.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Your Charge phase, just after a Death Guard unit from your army ends a Charge move. TARGET: That DEATH GUARD unit. EFFECT: Each enemy unit within Engagement Range of your unit must take a Battle-shock test, subtracting 1 from that test.

detachmentAbilities.name: Worldblight
detachmentAbilities.legend: As the foul tendrils of the Death Guard quest further across the battlefield, they infest even the most secure, holy and heavily warded sites with supernatural disease. To fight amidst such foulness is a virtual death sentence to the foe, win or lose.
detachmentAbilities.description: If you control an objective marker at the end of your Command phase and a Death Guard unit from your army (excluding Battle-shocked units) is within range of that objective marker, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase. In addition, until you lose control of that objective marker, it has the Nurgle’s Gift ability as if it were a DEATH GUARD model from your army.

detachmentAbilities.name: Manifold Maladies
detachmentAbilities.legend: A spirit of competition infects Nurgle’s champions when they fight alongside one another. With each vying to outdo the others in virulence, the foes are subjected to a riotous string of nightmarish outbreaks amongst their ranks.
detachmentAbilities.description: At the start of the battle round, you can select one of the Plagues listed in Nurgle’s Gift. Until the end of the battle, that is your chosen Plague instead of any previously chosen Plague.

detachmentAbilities.name: Reverberant Rancidity
detachmentAbilities.legend: Reality rings like a struck bell as it rebels against the sheer wrongness of Nurgle’s daemons. Borne upon these jarring waves of metaphysical disease, magnified and redoubled with every dolorous peal, the Plague God’s myriad infections spread and multiply all the faster.
detachmentAbilities.description: While a Plague Legions unit from your army is within 7" of one or more DEATH GUARD units from your army, that PLAGUE LEGIONS unit has the Nurgle’s Gift ability. While a Death Guard unit from your army is within 7" of one or more PLAGUE LEGIONS units from your army, add 3" to that DEATH GUARD units Contagion Range. PLAGUE LEGIONS You can include Plague Legions units in your army, even though they do not have the DEATH GUARD Faction keyword. The combined points cost of such units you can include in your army is: Incursion: Up to 500 pts Strike Force: Up to 1000 pts Onslaught: Up to 1500 pts No PLAGUE LEGIONS models from your army can be your WARLORD.

detachmentAbilities.name: Deadly Vectors
detachmentAbilities.legend: The plagues and parasites to which these elite warriors play host are amongst the most lethal in the galaxy. Moment by moment, even the most resilient foes sicken and wither in their presence until all that remains is rotted matter deliquescing sloppily from mouldering bones.
detachmentAbilities.description: In your opponent’s Command phase, roll 2D6 for each Afflicted enemy unit, subtracting 1 from the result if that unit is Below Half-strength. If the result is 6 or less, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds.

detachmentAbilities.name: Miasmic Bombardment
detachmentAbilities.legend: In the wake of preparatory bombardment by artillery weapons both forbidden and foul, the surviving foe are left reeling amidst clouds of plague smog. With their flesh running like tallow and their weapons rusting in their hands, they will be hard-pressed to fight back.
detachmentAbilities.description: At the start of the battle round, select a number of enemy units more than 12" away from every model from your army that is on the battlefield. Until the end of the battle round, those enemy units are Afflicted. The maximum number of units you can select in this way depends on the battle size, as shown below. BATTLE SIZEUNITSIncursion1Strike Force2Onslaught3

detachmentAbilities.name: Numberless Horde
detachmentAbilities.legend: No matter how many Poxwalkers the enemy gun down or tear limb-from-limb at close quarters, more groaning mutants keep coming from every side.
detachmentAbilities.description: In your Command phase in each of the following battle rounds, depending on your chosen battle size, add a new Poxwalkers unit with a Starting Strength of 10 to your army, in Strategic Reserves. BATTLE SIZEBATTLE ROUNDSIncursion2,3Strike Force2, 3, 4Onslaught2, 3, 4, 5 KEYWORDS POXWALKERS units from your army gain the BATTLELINE keyword.

datasheets.name: Spartan
datasheets.loadout: This model is equipped with: 2 quad lascannons; twin heavy bolter; armoured tracks.
datasheets.transport: This model has a transport capacity of 28 Death Guard Infantry models. Each Jump Pack, Wulfen, Gravis, Possessed or Terminator model takes up the space of 2 models and each Centurion or Obliterator model takes up the space of 3 models.

options.description: This model’s 2 quad lascannons can be replaced with 2 laser destroyers.
This model’s twin heavy bolter can be replaced with 1 twin heavy flamer
This model can be equipped with one of the following:1 heavy bolter 1 heavy flamer 1 multi-melta 1 storm bolter

abilities.name: Deadly Demise
abilities.description: DEADLY DEMISEFrom detonating ammo stores to corrosive innards or frenzied death throes, some targets are deadly even in defeat. Some models have ‘Deadly Demise x’ listed in their abilities. When such a model is destroyed, roll one D6 before removing it from play (if such a model is a TRANSPORT, roll before any embarked models disembark). On a 6, each unit within 6" of that model suffers a number of mortal wounds denoted by ‘x’ (if this is a random number, roll separately for each unit within 6"). Example: A TRANSPORT model with the Deadly Demise D3 ability is destroyed. Before any models disembark and before removing it from play, its controlling player rolls one D6, getting a 6. There are three units within 6" of that destroyed model, so its controlling player rolls one D3 for each of them, inflicting mortal wounds accordingly. Deadly Demise x: When this model is destroyed, roll one D6. On a 6, each unit within 6" suffers ‘x’ mortal wounds.
abilities.legend: From detonating ammo stores to corrosive innards or frenzied death throes, some targets are deadly even in defeat.

abilities.name: Nurgle’s Gift (Aura)
abilities.description: If your Army Faction is DEATH GUARD, while an enemy unit is within Contagion Range of one or more Death Guard models from your army, it is Afflicted (see below). CONTAGION RANGE Contagion Range changes over the course of the battle: AFFLICTED During the Declare Battle Formations step, select one of the Plagues below. Until the end of the battle, while an enemy unit is Afflicted, subtract 1 from the Toughness characteristic of models in that unit, and that unit has the effect of your chosen Plague.Skullsquirm Blight This horrifying affliction sees the minds and senses of the victim gnawed gradually away from within. Each time a model in this unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll. Rattlejoint Ague Limbs shuddering with fever palsy, bones turned brittle as glass, these warriors can barely act to defend themselves. Worsen the Save characteristic of models in this unit by 1. Scabrous Soulrot Victims of this insidious ailment become glassy eyed and morose as their very animus and essence decay. Worsen the Move, Leadership, and Objective Control characteristics of models in this unit by 1 (this rule can only worsen a model’s Objective Control characteristic to a minimum of 1).
abilities.legend: The Death Guard are warriors of the Plague God Nurgle. Their bloated bodies are riddled with corruption, and their mere presence causes the foe to sicken and wither in the grip of supernatural diseases while the battlefield falls to rot and ruin around them.

abilities.name: Assault Ramp
abilities.description: Each time a unit disembarks from this model after it has made a Normal move, that unit is still eligible to declare a charge this turn.

stratagems.legend: Attempting to counterattack Death Guard can leave foes floundering amidst bubbling filth.

EFFECT: Until the end of the phase, each time an enemy unit selects your unit as the target of a charge, subtract 2 from the Charge roll (this is not cumulative with any other negative modifiers to that Charge roll).

TARGET: That unit or model from your army. EFFECT: You re-roll that roll, test or saving throw.

stratagems.legend: In close-quarters combat, the slightest hesitation can leave an opening for a swift foe to exploit.
stratagems.description: WHEN: Fight phase, just after an enemy unit has fought. TARGET: One unit from your army that is within Engagement Range of one or more enemy units and that has not already been selected to fight this phase. EFFECT: Your unit fights next.
